title: Codex Uncertainty Clarification Verdict
description: One-shot codex consultation invoked by the Claude orchestrator immediately before any ASK_HUMAN escalation that stems from model-vs-model uncertainty (not from a categorical safety veto). Output PROCEED to break the tie with a concrete chosen option; output ESCALATE to surface the unresolved uncertainty to the human.

verdict.description: PROCEED only when the clarification can confidently pick a path forward AND no high-risk category is in play. ESCALATE in every other case.

chosen_option.description: When PROCEED, the option id (or short label) the orchestrator should take. Null when ESCALATE.

reasoning.description: Plain-language explanation. Must address (a) why the disagreement existed, (b) what evidence resolves it, and (c) why the chosen path is safe.

residual_concerns.description: Concerns that remain even if PROCEED — these get logged to state and flagged in any future final-alignment audit. Empty array means none.

high_risk_unresolved.description: True iff any of the categories (security boundary, data loss, irreversible migration, regulatory/clinical, external API contract, production credentials) is in play and is NOT fully resolved by the chosen option. When true, orchestrator MUST escalate even if verdict says PROCEED — verdict is overridden.

confidence.description: Codex's self-rated confidence in the verdict. Orchestrator policy: confidence < 0.75 → treat as ESCALATE regardless of verdict field.
